title: Training School Programme
text: The training school will take place over five days, beginning with theoretical information, structured teaching and hands on activities and allowing time towards the end of the week to apply practices to your own datasets.

title: Training School Programme
text:
Outline
The training school will begin by instructing participants in the foundational concepts of Linked Open Data (LOD) and semantic mapping for cultural heritage, covering key principles, benefits, and relevant standards and controlled vocabularies. Participants will learn about the development and structure of the ARIADNE portal and the data standard that underpins it - the AO-Cat ontology, a subset of CIDOC-CRM framework.
The remainder of the training school will be dedicated to developing practical skills for data mapping, by using ARIADNE’s mapping tools and resources including the X3ML Toolkit and Vocabulary mapping tool, as well as related software such as Open Refine. By the end of the training week, participants will have gained all the skills they need to map their archaeological and heritage data to the ARIADNE portal.
No previous experience is necessary, however, participants will be required to have a real-world dataset that they would like to map to the ARIADNE Portal. The workshop will use example datasets to train participants, which will lead them to map their own data to the ARIADNE portal with support from staff from the ADS and other experts.
This page provides the preliminary programme for the training school, including the topics covered. Further details will be added below as topics and events are finalised.

title: Training School Programme
text:
🗓️ Schedule
Training begins each day at 9.30am and finishes at 4pm. Coffee breaks will take place in the ADS Offices. Lunch is not provided but there are a number of cafes and restaurants are in close proximity.

Monday 12th October - Introduction


Tuesday 13th October - Linked Open Data and Semantic Mapping


Wednesday 14th October - The ARIADNE Portal, CIDOC-CRM and the AO-Cat Ontology


Thursday 15th October - Mapping Tools - Practical sessions


Friday 16th October - Mapping Sessions continued

title: ADS Transnational Access Training School
section: Linked Open Data and Semantic Mapping: Publishing your archaeological data to ARIADNE
text:
Linked Open Data and Semantic Mapping: Publishing your archaeological data to ARIADNE
This five day training school will instruct participants with the understanding and practical skills needed for semantic data mapping of archaeological and heritage data. This training school will focus on how to map data and aggregate it to the ARIADNE portal, a central point of access to digital archives and online resources related to archaeology and heritage from throughout Europe.
No previous experience is necessary, however, participants will be required to have a real-world dataset that they would like to map to the ARIADNE Portal.
Topics covered in this training school will include:

An Introduction to Linked Open Data
The principles of semantic data integration
The ARIADNE Portal, CIDOC-CRM and the AO-Cat Ontology
Preparing your data to map to the ARIADNE Portal
How to use ARIADNE’s mapping tools and resources

title: ADS Transnational Access Training School
text:
Training School 2026
📆 Date 12 to 16 October 2026
🌍 Location Guildhall, The Courtyard, Coney St, York YO1 9QN
Please see the Information page for more details.

The training school is provided as part of the ATRIUM Project’s Transnational Access scheme and is hosted by the Archaeology Data Service, a digital repository hosted by the Department of Archaeology at the University of York.
This annual program will run from 2024 through 2026, welcoming up to 10 participants each year for an intensive one-week training experience.

title: The Archaeology Data Service
text: The Archaeology Data Service (ADS) is the leading accredited repository in the UK for archaeology and historic environment data. With over 25 years of experience the ADS supports research, learning and teaching with free, high quality and dependable digital resources.

title: The Archaeology Data Service
text:
👩‍🏫 Your instructors
Dr Nicky Garland  0000-0002-6789-0779
Nicky is the Training and Communications Manager at the Archaeology Data Service (ADS). Nicky is an archaeologist with experience of teaching and training in both developer-led archaeology and academia.
Nicky is the course convenor for this Training School and the contact1 for all of your questions.
Dr Émilie Pagé-Perron  0000-0002-9878-9200
Émilie is a Research Associate at the ADS. Émilie is the lead for metadata workflows as part of the ATRIUM project.
Dr Sarah Middle
Sarah is a Post-doctoral Research Associate at the ADS. Sarah provides research and project management support across major European cultural heritage infrastructure projects, including ARTEMIS, AUTOMATA and ECHOES.
Dr Tim Evans  0000-0002-2725-6730
Tim is the Deputy Director of the ADS. Tim holds responsibility for all day-to-day operational issues at the ADS, and alongside our Director Prof Julian Richards is responsible for strategic development.
Dr Holly Wright  0000-0002-3403-4159
Holly is the Research Projects Manager of the ADS. Holly manages and implements the ADS contribution to a range of major international research projects.
Dr Ewan Chipping
Ewan is the HSDS Training Officer. He is responsible for designing and delivering training to users of the service.

ATRIUM
Advancing FronTier Research In the Arts and hUManities (ATRIUM) is a European Commission funded project that aims to exploit and strengthen interconnections between leading European infrastructures: (DARIAH, ARIADNE, CLARIN, OPERAS) to improve access to a portfolio of state-of-the-art services available to researchers across countries, languages, domains and media. This project will build on a shared understanding and interoperability principles established in the SSHOC (Social Sciences & Humanities Open Cloud) cluster project and other previous collaborations.
For more information about ATRIUM please visit the project website.

title: Applications
text: The ADS Training School is part of the ATRIUM Transnational Access (TNA) Scheme. More information about the scheme can be found on the ATRIUM website on the TNA pages.

title: Applications
text:
✍️ Apply here
ATRIUM’s Transnational Access (TNA) scheme offers researchers the possibility to apply for a fully funded placement at a number of different partner organisations to access expert knowledge and advice from leading Data Management organisations across Europe. The ADS Training School is one of a number available via the TNA scheme. For a full list please see the list of Host Organisations at the bottom of the TNA webpages.






Tip



Applications to the ADS Training School should be made through this online form

title: Applications
text:
Application Process
For more information about eligibility requirements, the information required and Frequently Asked Questions can be found on the Application pages.






Important



Only applicants with organisational affiliations outside of the UK are eligible to receive funding.


If you are successful in your application you are required to:

Write a report about your visit.
Provide feedback to the ADS on training school after your visit.
Provide ATRIUM with the details of any outputs resulting from the TNA scheme.

title: Information

🌍 Location
The session will be held in person at the main offices of the Archaeology Data Service. Please see the map below for the exact location.
The address of the ADS offices are:

Guildhall, The Courtyard, Coney St, York YO1 9QN

title: Information
text:
📖 Expenses
The ADS Training School is facilitated through the ATRIUM project Transnational Access Scheme. By applying to this scheme expenses incurred through attending the training school will be reimbursed to successful applicants.
This scheme covers the following expenses:

International travel (including all travel costs within the UK)
Accommodation for the duration of the training school
Subsistence expenses

title: Information
text:
✈️ Travelling to York
The nearest airport to the University is Manchester Airport and there is a direct train from London from Kings Cross Station. Kings Cross is a short walk from St Pancras International Station, which is served by the Eurostar. For more information about travelling to York international please visit the ‘Arriving from Overseas’ webpage on the University of York website.
More information is accessible via the Univeristy of York’s travel webpages.

title: Information
text:
🏨 Accommodation
York has a number of choices for accommodation in and around the city. Please look at the Visit York website for more information. If you are accepted to the Training School, we would be happy to provide you with some suggestions about where to stay in York.
